description: The political debate surrounding the China-Australia Free Trade Agreement (ChAFTA), signed in 2015. The agreement sparked intense public concern regarding labor market competition and foreign ownership, creating a sharp polarization between business groups and unions.

key_events.description: Construction, Forestry, Maritime, Mining and Energy Union launches anti-ChAFTA advertising campaign.
Government releases legal analysis refuting claims regarding visa sub-classes.
Labor Party reaches an agreement with the Government on side-letters for labor protections.
Parliament passes legislation implementing the agreement.

calibration_notes: Exemplifies high polarization scenarios where union involvement causes a temporary dip in support before institutional compromise.
